articles.title: WHY ONE THIRD OF ALL STARTUPS ARE SOLO FOUNDED
articles.summary: Solo founders now launch 36% of new startups, up from under 25% in 2019, marking the first time in over 50 years that a single founder builds more than a third of new companies. AI tools let one person handle coding, design, marketing, analytics, and support, enabling solo founders to compete with multi-person teams. Investors are increasingly backing solo founders because singular decision-making preserves “authorship,” creating coherent products and faster execution while avoiding co-founder conflicts.

articles.title: MODEL-MARKET FIT
articles.summary: Product-market fit has a prerequisite most AI founders ignore. Before the market can pull your product, the model has to actually be capable of doing the job. Legal AI was stuck for years until GPT-4 crossed the threshold, then minted more unicorns in 18 months than the previous decade. You can tell when MMF is missing by how human-in-the-loop gets positioned. When it exists, human oversight is a feature. When it doesn't, it's a crutch. The dangerous zone is betting on capability that's 24-36 months away.

articles.title: MASTERING COLD EMAIL
articles.summary: Cold email still works, otherwise nobody would be doing it. The problem is that most people only understand adding volume when they should focus on messaging potency. For less than $1,000, you can personalize emails to 10,000 people in a day. The key insight is rethinking your offer because demos are dead. Nobody has time for them anymore. The best offers are valuable to the user, aligned with what you actually sell, and filter for good-fit customers.

articles.title: PERSONAL LAND-MINES DETERMINE STARTUP FAILURE
articles.summary: The biggest reason startups fail isn't market timing, competition, or tech risk, it's the founder's personal life. Divorce, co-founder breakups, substance issues, or shifting priorities are consistent “land-mines” that end companies. Early-stage investing isn't just underwriting an idea. It's underwriting a human over a long, unpredictable journey. Founder durability and momentum through life's pressures may matter more than almost any other factor in startup success.

articles.summary: OS Ninja is an AI-guided learning platform that turns open source repositories into interactive code exploration experiences.

articles.title: FLOWGENIE
articles.summary: FlowGenie is a no-code platform for building forms and automations with a visual, node-based workflow editor.

articles.title: WHY EVERY EXEC SHOULD BE VIBECODING
articles.summary: The role of leadership is changing. Pure leadership and management are no longer a full-time job. The future will see more seniors and executives working as part-time individual contributors. Vibecoding is a great way to experience the balance. Company leaders can't afford to miss out on this skillset.

articles.title: THE COMMODITIZATION OF SERVICES
articles.summary: Before electrification, light was scarce and expensive. When it became cheap, people stopped thinking about the cost of turning on a switch. We're at the same moment with AI agents. Services that cost hundreds of thousands will cost tens to hundreds. The current agency model and vertical SaaS both rely on friction that's disappearing. If the cost of production drops to near zero, price must follow.

articles.title: SCALING TEAMS: PEOPLE, PROJECTS, AND PROCESS
articles.summary: Good scaling requires thoughtfully addressing the specific constraints a team is facing, evolving approaches as the team and organization change, and always keeping the focus on people and delivering value effectively.

articles.title: THE POSTMODERN DATA STACK IS AI
articles.summary: The modern data stack works brilliantly for structured data, but businesses thrive on unstructured data, something AI excels in.
